title: Understanding the Organisation and Its Context
description: Determine external and internal issues relevant to the organisation's purpose that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcomes of its AI management system. Include issues related to AI-specific risks such as bias, transparency, explainability, and societal impact.
guidance: Document internal factors (AI strategy, existing ISMS, data governance maturity) and external factors (regulation, industry norms, public trust) that shape AI risk. Update at least annually and after significant AI deployments.
implementationGuide: Establish and document understanding the organisation and its context as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Understanding the Organisation and Its Context policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating understanding the organisation and its context is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Understanding the Needs and Expectations of Interested Parties
description: Determine interested parties relevant to the AI management system including employees, customers, regulators, affected individuals, and society. Identify their requirements and expectations in relation to AI use.
guidance: Map stakeholders to AI systems and document their expectations (fairness, accuracy, explainability, data rights). Feed outputs into the AI risk assessment process.
implementationGuide: Establish and document understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Understanding the Needs and Expectations of Interested Parties policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Determining the Scope of the AI Management System
description: Determine the boundaries and applicability of the AI management system. Consider the context, interested parties, interfaces, and dependencies between the organisation and its AI systems.
guidance: Define scope covering which AI systems, business units, and use cases are in scope. Exclude only systems with documented justification. Publish the scope statement to relevant stakeholders.
implementationGuide: Establish and document determining the scope of the ai management system as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Determining the Scope of the AI Management System policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating determining the scope of the ai management system is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Management System
description: Establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve the AI management system in accordance with ISO/IEC 42001. Integrate with existing management systems where appropriate.
guidance: Integrate AIMS with existing ISMS (ISO 27001) and quality management systems. Appoint an AI management system owner and establish a governance committee.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai management system as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Management System policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai management system is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Leadership and Commitment
description: Top management must demonstrate leadership and commitment to the AI management system by ensuring the AI policy is established, integrating AIMS requirements into business processes, and promoting a culture of responsible AI.
guidance: Obtain board-level sponsorship for the AIMS. Include AI governance as a standing agenda item for senior leadership meetings. Document management review outputs.
implementationGuide: Establish and document leadership and commitment as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Leadership and Commitment policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating leadership and commitment is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Policy
description: Top management must establish, implement, and maintain an AI policy that is appropriate to the organisation, includes a commitment to responsible AI development and use, and is communicated to all relevant parties.
guidance: Draft an AI policy covering commitments to fairness, transparency, accountability, human oversight, and compliance. Obtain CEO/board sign-off. Publish internally and summarise publicly.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai policy as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Policy policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai policy is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Organisational Roles, Responsibilities and Authorities
description: Top management must assign and communicate roles and responsibilities for the AI management system including those responsible for AI development, deployment, monitoring, and incident response.
guidance: Define an AI governance RACI covering the AI Ethics Board, AI System Owner, AI Risk Manager, Data Scientist, and Legal/Compliance. Integrate with HR job descriptions.
implementationGuide: Establish and document organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Organisational Roles, Responsibilities and Authorities policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Actions to Address Risks and Opportunities
description: Determine risks and opportunities that need to be addressed by the AI management system. Consider risks from AI errors, bias, misuse, security, and societal harm alongside opportunities from responsible AI.
guidance: Conduct an AI-specific risk assessment covering technical risks (model failure, data poisoning), ethical risks (bias, discrimination), and compliance risks (AI Act, GDPR). Document treatment plans.
implementationGuide: Establish and document actions to address risks and opportunities as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Actions to Address Risks and Opportunities policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating actions to address risks and opportunities is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Risk Assessment Process
description: Establish and apply an AI risk assessment process to identify, analyse, and evaluate AI-related risks. The process must include criteria for acceptable risk levels and produce consistent, comparable, and reproducible results.
guidance: Define AI risk assessment criteria aligned to impact (privacy, safety, fairness, security) and likelihood. Conduct assessments before each new AI deployment and after significant model changes.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai risk assessment process as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Risk Assessment Process policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai risk assessment process is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Risk Treatment
description: Select and implement appropriate risk treatment options for identified AI risks. Document an AI risk treatment plan and obtain approval from risk owners.
guidance: For each identified risk, select treatment: mitigate (technical controls), accept (with documented rationale), transfer (insurance/contract), or avoid (discontinue use case). Track treatment plans in a risk register.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai risk treatment as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Risk Treatment policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai risk treatment is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Objectives and Planning to Achieve Them
description: Establish AI objectives at relevant functions and levels that are consistent with the AI policy, measurable, and monitored. Document plans for achieving AI objectives including responsibilities and timelines.
guidance: Define measurable AI objectives such as model accuracy thresholds, bias metrics, explainability scores, and incident response times. Review quarterly against actuals.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai objectives and planning to achieve them as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Objectives and Planning to Achieve Them policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai objectives and planning to achieve them is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Resources
description: Determine and provide resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance, and continual improvement of the AI management system. Include people, infrastructure, technology, and financial resources.
guidance: Allocate budget for AI governance tooling, model monitoring, bias testing, and training. Document resource requirements in the AIMS business case.
implementationGuide: Establish and document resources as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Resources policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating resources is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Competence
description: Determine the necessary competence of persons doing work that affects AI performance. Ensure persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience.
guidance: Define competency requirements for AI developers, reviewers, and operators. Deliver training on responsible AI principles, bias detection, and AI system lifecycle. Track completion.
implementationGuide: Establish and document competence as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Competence policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating competence is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Awareness
description: Ensure relevant persons are aware of the AI policy, their contribution to the AIMS, the benefits of improved AI management, and the implications of not conforming to requirements.
guidance: Deliver AI governance awareness training to all staff who use or interact with AI systems. Include case studies of AI failures and their consequences.
implementationGuide: Establish and document awareness as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Awareness policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating awareness is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Communication
description: Determine internal and external communications relevant to the AI management system including what to communicate, when, to whom, and how.
guidance: Document an AI communication plan. Communicate AI decisions, model changes, and incidents to relevant stakeholders. Publish AI system transparency notices for affected parties.
implementationGuide: Establish and document communication as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Communication policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating communication is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Documented Information
description: The AI management system must include documented information required by the standard and determined by the organisation as necessary for the effectiveness of the AIMS.
guidance: Maintain a document register for AIMS documents including AI policy, risk assessments, impact assessments, model cards, data sheets, and audit reports. Use version control.
implementationGuide: Establish and document documented information as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Documented Information policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating documented information is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Operational Planning and Control
description: Plan, implement, control, and maintain processes needed to meet requirements and implement actions identified in planning. Control planned changes and review unintended changes.
guidance: Define operational procedures for all AI system lifecycle phases: design, development, testing, deployment, monitoring, and decommissioning. Enforce change management.
implementationGuide: Establish and document operational planning and control as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Operational Planning and Control policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating operational planning and control is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Risk Assessment (Operational)
description: Perform AI risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed. Retain documented information of the results.
guidance: Conduct AI risk assessments before deploying new AI systems, after significant model updates, and annually for production systems. Document and retain all assessment records.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai risk assessment (operational) as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Risk Assessment (Operational) policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months

title: AI Risk Treatment (Operational)
description: Implement the AI risk treatment plan. Retain documented information of the results of the AI risk treatment.
guidance: Implement treatment controls per the AI risk treatment plan. Validate that controls operate effectively before system go-live. Document evidence of implementation.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai risk treatment (operational) as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai risk treatment (operational) is operational

title: AI System Impact Assessment
description: Conduct an AI system impact assessment to evaluate the potential impacts of AI systems on individuals, groups, and society.
guidance: For each AI system with significant decision-making capability, conduct an impact assessment covering fairness, privacy, safety, and societal implications. Review before deployment and annually.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai system impact assessment as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI System Impact Assessment policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months

title: Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis and Evaluation
description: Evaluate the performance and effectiveness of the AI management system. Retain documented information as evidence of results.
guidance: Define KPIs for the AIMS: model performance metrics, bias scores, incident rates, training completion rates. Measure and report monthly to senior management.
implementationGuide: Establish and document monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis and Evaluation policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Internal Audit
description: Conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the AIMS conforms to requirements and is effectively implemented.
guidance: Establish an annual AI governance internal audit programme. Use audit checklists aligned to ISO 42001 clauses. Assign auditors who are independent of the AI systems they audit.
implementationGuide: Establish and document internal audit as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Internal Audit policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating internal audit is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Management Review
description: Top management must review the AI management system at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness.
guidance: Schedule a bi-annual AIMS management review. Include inputs: audit results, risk register status, AI incident summary, stakeholder feedback, and performance against objectives.
implementationGuide: Establish and document management review as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Management Review policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating management review is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Continual Improvement
description: Continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the AI management system.
guidance: Track AIMS improvement actions in a register. Review progress quarterly. Use internal audit findings, management review outputs, and AI incident post-mortems as inputs.
implementationGuide: Establish and document continual improvement as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Continual Improvement policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating continual improvement is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Nonconformity and Corrective Action
description: When a nonconformity occurs, take action to control and correct it and address the consequences. Determine the cause and implement corrective action. Review effectiveness.
guidance: Define a nonconformity procedure for AI-related failures: model errors causing harm, biased outputs, data breaches affecting AI systems. Log all nonconformities and track corrective actions to closure.
implementationGuide: Establish and document nonconformity and corrective action as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Nonconformity and Corrective Action policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating nonconformity and corrective action is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Policies Relating to AI
description: Establish, implement, maintain, and communicate policies relating to AI that are appropriate to the organisation's context and risk profile.
guidance: Define topic-specific AI policies covering acceptable use, prohibited use cases, high-risk AI deployment criteria, and human oversight requirements.
implementationGuide: Establish and document policies relating to ai as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

title: Process and Controls Relating to AI
description: Establish processes and controls relating to AI systems to ensure responsible development and use.
guidance: Implement AI-specific controls across the lifecycle: model governance, data quality gates, fairness testing, explainability requirements, and human-in-the-loop thresholds.

title: Internal Organisation
description: Establish a governance structure for managing AI within the organisation including defined roles and responsibilities.
guidance: Establish an AI Ethics/Governance Committee with defined terms of reference. Include representation from legal, risk, technology, and business units.

title: AI Roles and Responsibilities
description: Define and communicate roles and responsibilities for AI system development, deployment, operation, and oversight.
guidance: Assign named AI System Owners for each production AI system. Define accountabilities in job descriptions and onboarding materials.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai roles and responsibilities as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

title: Data Quality and Data Governance
description: Ensure that data used in AI systems is of sufficient quality and that data governance processes are in place.
guidance: Implement data quality checks in the ML pipeline: completeness, accuracy, consistency, and representativeness. Document data provenance and lineage for all training datasets.
implementationGuide: Establish and document data quality and data governance as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

title: Data Provenance and Lineage
description: Maintain records of data provenance and lineage for AI training and operational data.
guidance: Use data cataloguing tools (Alation, Collibra, or dbt) to track data lineage from source to model. Document in model cards.
implementationGuide: Establish and document data provenance and lineage as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

description: Manage resources required for AI system development, training, deployment, and operation including compute, data, and personnel.
guidance: Define resource allocation processes for AI projects. Track compute costs, data storage, and staffing requirements per AI system.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai system resources management as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

guidance: Define an impact assessment template covering fairness, privacy, safety, security, and societal impact. Require completion before any AI system goes to production.

description: Consider potential impacts of AI systems on human rights and fundamental freedoms including non-discrimination, privacy, and freedom of expression.
guidance: Assess AI systems for potential discriminatory outcomes by protected characteristics. Engage legal counsel to review use cases in regulated domains.
implementationGuide: Establish and document human rights and fundamental freedoms assessment as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

guidance: Include societal impact dimensions in the AI impact assessment: labour displacement, environmental cost, and potential for weaponisation or discrimination at scale.
implementationGuide: Establish and document societal and ethical impact assessment as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

guidance: Establish a model development standard requiring fairness metrics, adversarial robustness testing, and explainability documentation before model approval.

description: Validate and test AI systems before deployment to ensure they meet defined requirements for accuracy, fairness, robustness, and safety.

title: AI System Decommissioning
description: Establish a process for decommissioning AI systems including notification of affected parties, secure disposal of models and data, and documentation of lessons learned.

title: Data Collection and Acquisition
description: Ensure that data collected or acquired for AI systems is appropriate, representative, and collected in accordance with applicable laws and ethical principles.
guidance: Define data collection requirements specifying representativeness, consent, and legal basis. Review data sources before use in AI training.

title: Data Pre-processing and Feature Engineering
description: Implement controls over data pre-processing and feature engineering to prevent the introduction of bias or quality degradation.
guidance: Document all data transformations in a pre-processing pipeline. Perform bias analysis at the feature level before training. Version-control datasets.

title: Data for Testing and Validation
description: Ensure that data used for AI system testing and validation is appropriate, representative, and separate from training data.
guidance: Maintain strict train/validation/test splits. Use representative holdout datasets for final model evaluation. Prevent data leakage between splits.
implementationGuide: Establish and document data for testing and validation as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

title: Transparency and Communication About AI Use
description: Provide appropriate information to interested parties about the use of AI systems including the nature of AI decision-making and how it affects them.
guidance: Publish AI transparency notices where AI is used in decisions affecting individuals. Include information on the AI system purpose, inputs, outputs, and appeal process.
implementationGuide: Establish and document transparency and communication about ai use as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.

guidance: Produce a model card for each production AI system documenting: purpose, training data, performance metrics, limitations, and known biases. Store in the model registry.

title: Documentation for Users and Affected Individuals
description: Provide appropriate documentation to users and affected individuals about AI system functionality, limitations, and recourse mechanisms.
guidance: Publish plain-language user guides for AI-assisted products. Include information on limitations, how to contest AI decisions, and escalation paths.
implementationGuide: Establish and document documentation for users and affected individuals as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Documentation for Users and Affected Individuals policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating documentation for users and affected individuals is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Intended Use and Acceptable Use Policy
description: Define the intended use of each AI system and establish an acceptable use policy for AI within the organisation.
guidance: Document intended use cases and explicitly prohibited use cases for each AI system. Publish an internal AI acceptable use policy signed off by legal and ethics teams.
implementationGuide: Establish and document intended use and acceptable use policy as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Intended Use and Acceptable Use Policy policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating intended use and acceptable use policy is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Human Oversight of AI Systems
description: Implement appropriate human oversight mechanisms for AI systems particularly those making or informing high-stakes decisions.
guidance: Define human-in-the-loop requirements per risk tier. For high-risk AI, require human review before acting on AI recommendations. Log all override decisions.
implementationGuide: Establish and document human oversight of ai systems as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Human Oversight of AI Systems policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating human oversight of ai systems is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Addressing Harmful or Inaccurate AI Outputs
description: Establish processes to detect, report, and address harmful, inaccurate, or biased AI outputs.
guidance: Implement feedback mechanisms for users to flag incorrect or harmful outputs. Define triage and remediation procedures. Track and report incidents in the AI incident register.
implementationGuide: Establish and document addressing harmful or inaccurate ai outputs as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Addressing Harmful or Inaccurate AI Outputs policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating addressing harmful or inaccurate ai outputs is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: Third-Party AI Systems and Services
description: Establish controls for the use of third-party AI systems and services including due diligence, contractual requirements, and ongoing monitoring.
guidance: Conduct AI-specific due diligence before adopting third-party AI tools: review model transparency, bias testing evidence, and data processing agreements. Include AI requirements in supplier contracts.
implementationGuide: Establish and document third-party ai systems and services as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented Third-Party AI Systems and Services policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating third-party ai systems and services is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control

title: AI Supply Chain Risk Management
description: Manage risks in the AI supply chain including foundation models, datasets, tooling, and infrastructure providers.
guidance: Assess and document AI supply chain risks for each production AI system. Monitor for changes to foundation models, training data, or APIs that could affect system behaviour.
implementationGuide: Establish and document ai supply chain risk management as part of your AI management system. Assign accountability to a named role, integrate with existing ISMS processes where applicable, and review at least annually.
exampleEvidence: Documented AI Supply Chain Risk Management policy or procedure, signed by senior management, dated within the last 12 months
Evidence of implementation: records, logs, or tool outputs demonstrating ai supply chain risk management is operational
Internal audit or review record confirming compliance with this control
